Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

As with your other patch, every version of this patch has repeatedly failed the same tests:

 sanity test_53
 runtests
 conf-sanity test_78

There are at least 10 times that these tests have failed, and this patch is the only one that is failing these tests.  The sanity test_53 appears to be some kind of bug with the last_id contents, which is closely related to what this patch is changing.  The conf-sanity and runtests failures look like some kind of space leak, maybe caused by orphan cleanup issues.

In any case, resubmitting the patch multiple times is not going to make those failures go away.  Please take the time to actually look at the patch failures, rather than just resubmitting the patch.  We are all trying to make the code better, but landing patches that fix a tiny corner case but break the common usage does not help anyone.

Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 5:

Andreas - as I looking at past time - all fails was don't related to this patch. So strange to have it failed.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-2

> Andreas - as I looking at past time - all fails was don't related
 > to this patch. So strange to have it failed.

This is *exactly* why we don't allow patches to land with test failures, even if "fails was don't related to this patch".  If you actually look at the test results, this patch has failed exactly the same tests many times, and is the *only* patch that is failing them in this way in the last 3 months.  Please take a look at the results:

 https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/query?utf8=✓&warn[notice]=&test_set_script_id=f9516376-32bc-11e0-aaee-52540025f9ae&sub_test_script_id=09e2e57e-32be-11e0-b685-52540025f9ae&status[]=FAIL&window[start_date]=2018-06-01&window[end_date]=&commit=Update+results
 https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/query?utf8=✓&warn[notice]=&test_set_script_id=f946ba8e-32bc-11e0-aaee-52540025f9ae&sub_test_script_id=9ae144d2-6181-11e2-be04-52540035b04c&status[]=FAIL&window[start_date]=2018-06-01&window[end_date]=2018-09-04&commit=Update+results
 https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/query?utf8=%E2%9C%93&warn%5Bnotice%5D=&test_set_script_id=7f66aa20-3db2-11e0-80c0-52540025f9af&sub_test_script_id=d2cf9a16-286d-11e3-9f56-52540035b04c&status%5B%5D=FAIL&window%5Bstart_date%5D=2018-06-01&window%5Bend_date%5D=&commit=Update+results

All of the failures for "can not match last_seq/last_id for *OST*-osc-MDT0000" and "Space not all freed: now 8192kB, was 6144kB" are for test runs of this patch, and since this patch is changing the last_id handling, it is very likely that the failures are caused by this patch.

I haven't looked into *why* the tests are failing.  It is possible the failure isn't directly related to this patch and there is a bug in the tests, or it is possible there is another existing bug in the code that this fix is exposing, but it is most likely that there is actually a bug in this patch.  I don't know - that is your job as patch author to understand and fix.

In any case, we can't land a patch that is failing tests, regardless of the cause, otherwise *every* patch based on this one will also start failing the same tests, and the testing will be useless.

By making statements that equate to "the 28 test failures that only happen with this patch are not related, so strange" makes me believe that you do not take the testing seriously (see also my comments on patch https://review.whamcloud.com/32905).

We all want bugs in Lustre to be fixed, and I thank you for working to fix these bugs.  Landing a patch that may fix a very rare bug, but replaces it with a bug that is hit constantly doesn't make any sense.  

Please take the time to investigate these test failures more closely and submit a patch that passes.  Please add to your future commit comments:

 Test-Parameters: envdefinitions=ONLY=53 testlist=sanity,sanity,sanity
 Test-Parameters: envdefinitions=ONLY=53 testlist=sanity,sanity,sanity
 Test-Parameters: envdefinitions=ONLY=78 testlist=conf-sanity,conf-sanity
 Test-Parameters: envdefinitions=ONLY=78 testlist=conf-sanity,conf-sanity
 Test-Parameters: envdefinitions=ONLY=78 testlist=conf-sanity,conf-sanity
 Test-Parameters: testlist=runtests,runtests,runtests,runtests
 Test-Parameters: testlist=runtests,runtests,runtests,runtests

so that these tests are known to pass consistently and not just a single time.

This patch and https://review.whamcloud.com/32905 are marked -2, which will not be removed even if the patch is resubmitted or rebased until I'm confident that it is actually passing consistently.

parents.subject: LU-11654 mdd: check ucred before using it

commit.subject: LU-11020 osp: fix race during lov_objids update
commit.message:
LU-11020 osp: fix race during lov_objids update

First thread can be delayed due to reading from disk, so it
will completed after second thread and overwrite the on-disk
lov_objids data with an older OID for that OST.

If the transaction commits during this window and then the
MDS crashes, it is possible that the stale lov_objids results
in an OST object being deleted during MDS->OSS recovery that
should have been kept.

Use a single buffer shared between threads to store lov_objids
so that even if multiple threads are updating the lov_objids
file at once, the latest OID will be written to disk even if
the threads commit their transactions out of order.

Cray-bug-id: LUS-5841
Change-Id: I0984e5f55d569260c1219bf87c82423cc5b8589b
Signed-off-by: Alexey Lyashkov <c17817@cray.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/32867
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <bzzz@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
